skill.name: ckm-design
skill.description: Design assistant for brand systems, logos, corporate identity mockups, slide decks, banners, icons, and social media images. It includes reference guides, CSV design data, and Python scripts that can call Gemini APIs to generate image and SVG assets.
skill.summary: Create brand, logo, presentation, icon, banner, and social media design assets from structured design guidance.
skill.author: nextlevelbuilder

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported a very high score, but review found many false positives from design terminology, Markdown command examples, and public template URLs. The confirmed risks are legitimate but sensitive: scripts load local API keys, call Gemini services with prompts or images, and write generated assets to local paths. No prompt injection attempt or confirmed malicious intent was found.

medium_findings.title: External AI API Calls for Design Generation
medium_findings.description: The logo, CIP, and icon generators use Gemini API clients and send user prompts, brand context, and in CIP image-editing mode user-provided logo images to external Google AI services. This is core functionality, but users must understand that design inputs and uploaded brand assets may leave the local environment.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code initializes Gemini clients with API keys and calls generate_content for user supplied design prompts. This is expected functionality, but it confirms third-party data transfer risk.

medium_findings.title: Local Environment File Loading for API Keys
medium_findings.description: Multiple generators load .env files from the skill directory and from ~/.claude paths, then read GEMINI_API_KEY or GOOGLE_API_KEY. This is a common configuration pattern, but it accesses local secret material and should be disclosed.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The .env path list and os.environ assignments are direct evidence of local secret loading. The code does not show broad exfiltration, but it handles credentials.

medium_findings.title: Generated Asset Writes to User Selected Paths
medium_findings.description: The scripts create output directories and write generated PNG, SVG, or HTML files. This is expected for asset generation, but user-controlled output paths can overwrite local files if the user provides an existing path.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file write paths are visible and are part of the advertised export workflow. Risk depends on user selected output values, so severity is moderate rather than high.

low_findings.title: Documentation Command Examples Flagged as Execution
low_findings.description: Many external command findings are Markdown examples showing how a user can run Python scripts or Chrome screenshot commands. They are not executed automatically by the skill instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are inside documentation code fences or workflow instructions. They still deserve disclosure because users may run them, but the scanner overstated automatic execution risk.

low_findings.title: Keyword-Based Crypto, C2, and SAM Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography, C2 keyword, and Windows SAM alerts appear to come from design terms, color words, model names, and ordinary output labels. No evidence found of cryptographic routines, command-and-control behavior, or Windows credential database access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted review found design data, display labels, and routing documentation at representative flagged locations. No semantic evidence supports malware or credential-database behavior.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Public Resource URLs in Templates
low_findings.description: Template references include public CDN or image placeholder URLs for Chart.js, Google Fonts, and sample background images. This is low risk, but generated HTML can make network requests when opened unless assets are vendored.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible in HTML templates and documentation. They are not hidden endpoints, but they can cause normal browser network activity.

dangerous_patterns.title: Third-Party Generation API Usage
dangerous_patterns.description: Gemini generate_content calls process user design prompts and generated asset requests through an external service.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Direct API calls are present in generator scripts. This is intended behavior but is still a sensitive operational pattern.

dangerous_patterns.title: Home Directory Secret Configuration Reads
dangerous_patterns.description: The scripts read ~/.claude based .env files to locate Gemini or Google API keys.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The hidden home-directory paths and environment variable loading are explicit. The pattern is legitimate but sensitive.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local File Creation and Overwrite Capability
dangerous_patterns.description: Generated design assets are written to caller selected output paths and directories.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The write operations are straightforward and expected. Impact depends on user selected destination paths.

content.value_statement: Design work often requires many asset types and consistent brand rules. This skill combines structured design references, generation scripts, and workflows for logos, identity mockups, slides, banners, icons, and social images.
actual_capabilities: Routes design requests to logo, CIP, slide, banner, icon, social photo, and design system workflows.
Searches local CSV design data for logo styles, color palettes, industries, CIP deliverables, and mockup contexts.
Generates logo images and corporate identity mockups through Gemini image models when API keys are configured.
Generates SVG icon assets through Gemini text output and saves single, batch, or multi-size exports.
Provides HTML presentation and social image guidance, including dimensions, layout patterns, and screenshot export workflows.
Writes generated PNG, SVG, and HTML assets to local output paths selected by the user.
limitations: AI image and SVG generation requires GEMINI_API_KEY or GOOGLE_API_KEY and the google-genai package.
Generated brand assets may need human review for trademark, accessibility, and production quality.
Some workflows depend on external skills or tools, such as browser screenshot capture and design-system helpers.
Prompts, brand details, and optional logo images can be sent to external AI services.

use_cases.title: Launch a Startup Brand Kit
use_cases.description: Create logo directions, color ideas, identity mockups, and a presentation deck for an early brand concept.

use_cases.title: Produce Campaign Visuals
use_cases.description: Design banners and social media images for several platforms while keeping size and style rules consistent.

use_cases.title: Build Product UI Assets
use_cases.description: Generate icon concepts, design tokens, and supporting visual assets for a web or mobile interface.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Brand Direction
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a brand design direction for a modern fitness app named PulsePath. Include logo style, color palette, typography, and first asset recommendations.

prompt_templates.title: Generate Logo Variations
prompt_templates.prompt: Design logo concepts for a sustainable coffee brand. Use organic and minimalist styles, then explain which concepts are best for packaging and signage.

prompt_templates.title: Create Identity Mockups
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a corporate identity program for a fintech consulting firm. Include business card, letterhead, office signage, and presentation cover mockups.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Multi-Platform Campaign
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a complete campaign visual system for a SaaS launch across LinkedIn, X, Instagram, YouTube, and Google Ads. Include sizes, art direction, asset list, and export plan.

output_examples.input: Design a logo system for a healthcare analytics startup.
output_examples.output: A concise design brief with recommended logo styles, calming color palettes, typography guidance, symbol ideas, and next steps for generated variants.

output_examples.input: Create social images for a product launch on LinkedIn and Instagram.
output_examples.output: A platform-specific asset plan with dimensions, layout direction, headline treatment, image guidance, and screenshot export instructions.

output_examples.input: Generate SVG icons for dashboard navigation.
output_examples.output: A set of icon directions and saved SVG outputs in the requested style, color, and sizes when Gemini credentials are configured.

best_practices: Use non-sensitive brand briefs unless external AI processing is approved.
Review generated images and SVGs before production use.
Choose explicit output directories to avoid accidental file overwrites.
anti_patterns: Do not send confidential unreleased logos to external models without permission.
Do not treat generated branding as trademark cleared or legally reviewed.
Do not run screenshot or generation commands without checking paths and credentials.

faq.question: Does this skill generate real image files?
faq.answer: Yes. The included scripts can generate PNG logos, CIP mockups, SVG icons, and HTML presentation files when dependencies and API keys are configured.

faq.question: Does it require external services?
faq.answer: Yes for AI generation. Logo, CIP, and icon generators use Gemini APIs. Reference lookup and planning can work locally.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Are generated assets production ready?
faq.answer: They are useful drafts and mockups. Review them for quality, accessibility, brand fit, licensing, and trademark concerns.

faq.question: What data can leave my machine?
faq.answer: Prompts, brand context, and optional logo images may be sent to Gemini when generation scripts are used.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill uses local scripts, API keys, external AI services, and file writes. These are legitimate features but require user awareness.
